creator: Lucas Ropek
title: (No AI) Meta removes controversial AI feature on Instagram after backlash
dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
content: "Our intent was to provide a useful creative tool and to give people control over whether their public content could be referenced in this way," the company said in a blog post. "We've heard the feedback that this feature missed the mark, so it's no longer available."
contentSnippet: "Our intent was to provide a useful creative tool and to give people control over whether their public content could be referenced in this way," the company said in a blog post. "We've heard the feedback that this feature missed the mark, so it's no longer available."

title: (No AI) Meta turns off the Instagram feature that let users make AI deepfakes of public accounts
author: Jay Peters
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/meta-muse-image-instagram-tag.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Following significant backlash, Meta is turning off the feature it announced this week that let users generate AI images based on content from public Instagram accounts just by tagging them. The feature, as originally set up, meant that content from any public Instagram account could be used in AI creations without the account owner's permission.

"Earlier this week, we announced that one way for people to generate images in Meta AI is by @-mentioning public Instagram accounts that they want to reference," Meta says in an update to [a blog post](https://about.fb.com/news/2026/07/introducing-muse-image-meta-ai/) about its new Muse Image AI model. "Our intent was to provide a useful creative tool and to give pe …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/964416/meta-instagram-ai-muse-image-deepfakes)
contentSnippet:
Following significant backlash, Meta is turning off the feature it announced this week that let users generate AI images based on content from public Instagram accounts just by tagging them. The feature, as originally set up, meant that content from any public Instagram account could be used in AI creations without the account owner's permission.
"Earlier this week, we announced that one way for people to generate images in Meta AI is by @-mentioning public Instagram accounts that they want to reference," Meta says in an update to a blog post about its new Muse Image AI model. "Our intent was to provide a useful creative tool and to give pe …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Following significant backlash, Meta is turning off the feature it announced this week that let users generate AI images based on content from public Instagram accounts just by tagging them. The feature, as originally set up, meant that content from any public Instagram account could be used in AI creations without the account owner's permission. […]

title: (No AI) Apple sues OpenAI for allegedly stealing hardware secrets
author: Jay Peters
content:
![An image of Tim Cook](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/04/STK468_APPLE_ANTITRUST_CVIRGINIA_D.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Apple has sued OpenAI, alleging that engineers stole Apple secrets to advance the AI startup's hardware plans. In [its complaint](https://www.documentcloud.org/documents/28453238-complaint-for-trade-secret-misappropriation-and-breach-of-contract/), Apple says it uncovered "a pattern of theft of Apple's trade secrets by OpenAI employees who were formerly at Apple." In addition to OpenAI, the lawsuit also names IO Products - Jony Ive's hardware startup, which OpenAI [bought in 2025](https://www.theverge.com/news/703114/openai-io-jony-ive-sam-altman-ai-hardware) - along with two specific employees, Tang Tan (OpenAI's chief hardware officer) and Chang Liu (who joined OpenAI from Apple in January).

An Apple spokesperson shared [this statement with _9to5Mac_](https://9to5mac.com/2026/07/10/apple-sues-openai-trade-secret-theft/?extended-comments=1):

> At Apple, our teams are constantly developing breakthrough technologies to create the b …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/964350/apple-openai-lawsuit-trade-secrets)
contentSnippet:
Apple has sued OpenAI, alleging that engineers stole Apple secrets to advance the AI startup's hardware plans. In its complaint, Apple says it uncovered "a pattern of theft of Apple's trade secrets by OpenAI employees who were formerly at Apple." In addition to OpenAI, the lawsuit also names IO Products - Jony Ive's hardware startup, which OpenAI bought in 2025 - along with two specific employees, Tang Tan (OpenAI's chief hardware officer) and Chang Liu (who joined OpenAI from Apple in January).
An Apple spokesperson shared this statement with 9to5Mac:
At Apple, our teams are constantly developing breakthrough technologies to create the b …

Read the full story at The Verge.

In its complaint, Apple says it uncovered "a pattern of theft of Apple's trade secrets by OpenAI employees who were formerly at Apple." In addition to OpenAI, the lawsuit also names IO Products - Jony Ive's hardware startup, […]
